scenario: Server hung trying to acquire spinlock by third party module strmfs_mod
symptoms: Multiple soft lockup's observed where various vcpus are calling drain_all_pages()

workflow.thought: Set context to analyze the hung task PID 19227
Get backtrace of hung task to see call chain
workflow.observation: Task stuck in smp_call_function_many waiting for CPU

workflow.thought: Inspect per-CPU call function data for CPU 12
workflow.observation: Check csd and cpumask values for CPU 12

workflow.thought: Alternative way to examine call_function_data structure
Read cpumask value to see which CPUs are targeted
Convert cpumask to binary to identify busy CPUs
workflow.observation: Identify which CPU bits are set in mask

workflow.thought: Check call_single_queue for all CPUs to find pending work
workflow.action: p call_single_queue:all | grep -e per -e first | paste - -
workflow.observation: Find CPU with non-empty queue (CPU 13 has work)

workflow.thought: Get backtrace of busy CPU 13 to see what's blocking
workflow.observation: CPU 13 stuck in spinlock within strmfs_mod module

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for RCU stall messages
workflow.observation: Multiple RCU stalls detected on CPU 13

root_cause_analysis: The strm11 task was trying to acquire the spinlock inside the function dupb().
However, the function dupb() is part of a third party [strmfs_mod] kernel module.
